original: Aorun zhaoi, a Theropod dinosaur, dating from 161 million years ago, is discovered in China
Kilauea volcano on Big Island, Hawaii begins erupting forcing the evacuation of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park and residential areas
MLB LA Angels pitcherShohei Ohtanijoins NY Yankee legend Babe Ruth as only pitcher to strike out 500 batters and hit over 100 career home runs in 6-4 over the Cardinals in St. Louis
Solar eclipse (2m 07s) seen at Ugarit by Mesopotamian astronomers "On the day of the new moon, in the month of Hiyar, the Sun was put to shame, and went down in the daytime, with Mars in attendance."
Sport of geocaching begins with the first cache placed and the GPS coordinates posted on Usenet
Russian chess grandmasterGarry Kasparovbegins chess match with IBM supercomputer Deep Blue
First airplane lands at the geographic North Pole
A new range of mountains is discovered in Antarctica, with two peaks over 13,000 feet
American swimmerKatie Ledeckybreaks her own world record for the 6th time in the 800-meter freestyle event at the TYR Pro Swim Series in Fort Lauderdale, Florida
Manchester City's Norwegian strikerErling Haalandscores in a 3-0 win over West Ham to set a new record for most goals scored in a single Premier League season (35)
"Spider-Man", starringTobey Maguireas Peter Parker/Spider-Man and Willem Dafoe as Norman Osborn/The Green Goblin, premieres
Joe Murray, American cartoonist (Rocko's Modern Life), born in San Jose, California
TheAnne FrankHouse opens in Amsterdam, The Netherlands
English poetLord Byronswims across the dangerous Hellespont Strait in Turkey (modern day Dardanelles)
Doug Henning, Canadian magician, born in Winnipeg, Manitoba (d. 2000)
US Environmental Protection Agency takes first significant step against climate change by announcing new limits on hydrofluorocarbons (-85% over 15 years)
Chicago's Sears Tower, world's tallest building (443 m), tops out
The Palmetto Playground in Brooklyn is renamed afterBeastie Boys' rapperAdam Yauch
Canadian poet and physicianJohn McCraewrites the poem "In Flanders Fields" at Ypres on the Western Front
Tiger's Charlie Maxwell hits 4 consecutive HRs in a doubleheader
Edmond Halleyobserves total eclipse phenomenon "Baily's Beads"
1st firing of a US Viking rocket; reached 80 km
Albert Sacco Jr., American chemical engineer and astronaut (STS 73), born in Boston, Massachusetts
Ron Popeil, American inventor and TV personality who popularized the phrase "But wait, there's more!", born in New York City (d. 2021)[1]
New Hampshire's famous Old Man of the Mountain collapses
Margaret Mitchellwins the Pulitzer Prize for her novel "Gone with the Wind"
Pulitzer prize awarded toUpton Sinclairfor his novel "Dragon's Teeth" about the Nazi rise to power
Allies arrest German physicistWerner Heisenberg
1st African American lawyer, Macon B. Allen, admitted to the bar in Massachusetts
Maria Itkina, Russian athlete (9 sprint world records, 60m-400m), born in Roslavl, Russia (d. 2020)
Christa Vahlensieck runs female world record marathon (2:40:15.8)
Věra Čáslavská, Czech gymnast (7 Olympic gold 1964, 68), born in Prague, Czechoslovakia (d. 2016)
Bertha Benz [Cacilie Bertha Ringer], German inventor, automotive pioneer and wife of fellow automotive inventor Karl Benz, born in Pfrozheim, German Confederation (d. 1944)
Cyclone Fani strikes Odisha State, India and Bangladesh, killing 33 people after one of the biggest human evacuations ever - 1.2 million in India in 24 hours
World premiere of The Beatles' "Let it Be" documentary film directed by Michael Lindsay-Hogg, in New York City
Frankie Valli [Francesco Stephen Castelluccio], American singer (Four Seasons - "Sherry"; "Can't Take My Eyes Off Of You"), born in Newark, New Jersey
Sandi Toksvig, Danish-British comedian and radio presenter (The Great British Baking Show), born in Copenhagen, Denmark
First regular steam train passenger service in the US starts in South Carolina, with U.S.-built locomotive “The Best Friend of Charleston”
First non-stop North American transcontinental flight (NY-San Diego) is completed
MLB Cleveland IndianBobby Bondshits his 300th HR (2nd to have 300 HRs & 300 stolen bases)
Future Hockey HOF right wingCam Neelyinjures knee on a cheap shot by Ulf Samuelsson in Wales Conference Finals; injury limits him to only 162 games for remainder of NHL career; retires at 31
West Indies all set to lose the cricket test v Australia at Kingston till riots end game
Musical "The Fantasticks" by Harvey Schmidt and Tom Jones opens in Greenwich Village, NYC, becomes “the longest-running musical in the universe”
Septima Poinsette Clark, American educator and civil rights activist, born in CHarleston, South Carolina (d. 1987)
Jeffrey C. Hall, American geneticist (Nobel Prize 2017 behavioral rhythms of the fly), born in New York City
John Lewis, American jazz pianist, arranger, and composer (Modern Jazz Quartet, 1952-97), born in La Grange, Illinois (d. 2001)
Henry Cooper, English boxer (British, European, C'wealth heavyweight champion; famous defeats to Muhammad Ali 1963, 66), born in London, England (d. 2011)
José Torres, Puerto Rican boxer (world light-heavyweight champion), born in Ponce, Puerto Rico (d. 2009)
Nona Gaprindashvili, Georgian world women's chess champ (1962-78), born in Zugdidi, Georgian SSR, Soviet Union
Allen Wells, Scottish athlete (Olympic 100m gold 1980), born in Edinburgh, Scotland
Carlos De León, Puerto Rican boxer (WBC cruiserweight champion (1980–82, 83–85, 86–88, 89–90), born in Trujillo Alto, Puerto Rico (d. 2020)
George Paget Thomson, English physicist who demonstrated electron diffraction (Nobel 1937), born in Cambridge, England (d. 1975)[1]
Alfred Kastler, French physicist (Nobel 1966 - Hertzian resonances within atoms), born in Guebwiller, Alsace, German Empire (d. 1984)
DR Congo announces the end of the latest Ebola outbreak after three months with a death toll of six
Indian government confirms around 110 people have died in dust storms in Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh in the last 9 days
Australia beat West Indies to regain the Frank Worrell Cricket Trophy
Chelsea wins the 2014–15 English football Premier League
Fall Out Boy's breakthrough album "From Under the Cork Tree" is released through Island Records
Constitution of May 3 is proclaimed by the Great Sejm (Parliament) of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, the first modern constitution in Europe
The University of Athens is founded by King Otto of Greece - first modern university in the Eastern Mediterranean
